mechanism_narrative: Strong and accelerating retail sales indicate robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where RSAFS growth is above its 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which in turn drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as market participants react quickly to deteriorating conditions.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances following a 0.3 percentage point increase in UNRATE over three months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) alongside an increasing federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, which typically leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of periods where both inflation and the federal funds rate are rising significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) during an economic expansion signal robust investment and consumer confidence, which underpins broader economic growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. However, this effect diminishes during recessions.
failure_condition: If the correlation between HOUST and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p-value > 0.1) during periods of confirmed economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, indicating a strong economic backdrop that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of periods where INDPRO shows positive year-over-year growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in non-farm payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer income and confidence, which in turn supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as markets price in improved economic prospects.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances following a strong acceleration in PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance, increasing borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, which dampens economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth (GDPC1) and robust retail sales (RSAFS) signifies broad-based strength in both aggregate demand and consumer spending, creating an environment conducive to strong corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS year-over-year growth > 3%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 30% of periods where both GDPC1 and RSAFS show strong year-over-year growth.
